zh:
北欧神话是维京时代斯堪的纳维亚人的信仰世界，靠吟游诗人的口头传诵存续数百年，直到皈依基督教之后的13世纪，才由冰岛人写定为文字。两部核心文献都出自冰岛：佚名汇编的《诗体埃达》收录了《女占卜者的预言》《高人的箴言》等古歌，斯诺里·斯图鲁松的《散文埃达》则以问答体系统重述了从创世到末日的全部神话；此外还有《沃尔松格萨迦》等英雄萨迦与丹麦人萨克索的拉丁文《丹麦人的事迹》可资参照。

这个宇宙以白蜡巨树尤克特拉希尔为轴，九个世界悬系其间：奥丁、托尔所在的阿萨神域，人类居住的中庭，巨人盘踞的约顿海姆，还有雾中的死者之国。阿萨与华纳两大神族在远古战争后合流，与巨人族保持着通婚又厮杀的复杂关系；侏儒在地底锻造神器，诡计师洛基游走于神与巨人之间，播下末日的种子。

它最独特的气质在于：诸神有死，末日已定。奥丁穷尽智慧读到的预言，是自己葬身狼腹、世界沉入大海——诸神照样宴饮、备战，把注定失败的抵抗进行到底。这种冷冽的宿命感，加上大地将再度浮起的许诺，被后世一再重写：瓦格纳的《尼伯龙根的指环》、托尔金的中洲、今天的电影与游戏，都从这里取材。
en:
Norse mythology is the belief-world of Viking Age Scandinavia. It survived for centuries in the mouths of poets and was only written down in the 13th century, in Christian Iceland. Both core texts are Icelandic: the anonymous Poetic Edda preserves ancient lays such as the Voluspa and the Havamal, while Snorri Sturluson's Prose Edda retells the whole cycle, from the making of the world to its end, in systematic form. Around them stand the heroic sagas, above all the Volsunga saga, and the Latin Gesta Danorum of Saxo Grammaticus.

The cosmos hangs on the great ash Yggdrasil, with nine worlds among its branches and roots: Asgard of the gods, Midgard of humankind, Jotunheim of the giants, and the misty realm of the dead below. Two divine tribes, the Aesir and the Vanir, merged after a primeval war; the gods marry giants and kill them in roughly equal measure, dwarfs forge treasures underground, and the trickster Loki moves between all camps, sowing the seeds of the end.

What sets this mythology apart is its temper: the gods are mortal, and their doom is already written. Odin spends his wisdom learning the prophecy of his own death in the wolf's jaws, then feasts and arms his warriors anyway, fighting a battle he knows is lost. That cold fatalism, paired with the promise that the earth will rise green from the sea a second time, has been retold ever since, from Wagner's Ring cycle to Tolkien's Middle-earth to the films and games of the present day.
